leadText: Wimpod (Japanese: ã‚³ã‚½ã‚¯ãƒ ã‚· Kosokumushi) is a dual-type Bug/Water PokÃ©mon introduced in Generation VII. It evolves into Golisopod starting at level 30.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Wimpod's underside and legs Wimpod is a small, arthropod PokÃ©mon . Its main body is purple and is covered by silver plating across its back. There are four plates in total. The first plate covers half of its head but leaves room for its eyes. The upper lids of its eyes appear to be covered with additional plating. The three plates on its back overlap and each one has a single black triangle in the middle. It has a long spike on each side of its head that curves backward, large yellow eyes with two short extensions over them like eyelashes, and two flat antennae that extend over its back. There are eight short legs on its underside, and it has a long, prickly tail. This PokÃ©mon is cowardly by nature and wary of both noise and sudden movements. However, its curiosity will cause it to approach people or PokÃ©mon that are standing still. The flailing of its many legs can cause Wimpod to leave a sparkling clean path in its wake when it moves. It will spit a poisonous liquid when threatened. Wimpod tends to gather in swarms, constantly on the lookout for anything. They scatter instantly the moment they detect an enemy's presence. Wimpod sometimes accompanies its evolved form, Golisopod. The stench of this fluid warns others that danger is nearby. Wimpod is known to be a natural scavenger. Wimpod will eat and store anything, including garbage from the sea. This habit makes them highly valued as cleaners. [ 1 ] When it finds coins, Murkrow and Meowth go after it. Wimpod is the only known PokÃ©mon that can have Wimp Out as an Ability . Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Wimpod evolves into Golisopod . (For specifics on this PokÃ©mon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game dataâ†’Evolution data .) Generation VII Alola S M : #182 Alola US UM : #221 Kanto #â€” This PokÃ©mon has no PokÃ©dex entries in Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! . Sun This PokÃ©mon is a coward. As it desperately dashes off, the flailing of its many legs leaves a sparkling clean path in its wake. Moon Its habitat varies from beaches to seabeds. A natural scavenger, it will gleefully chow down on anything edible, no matter how rotten. Ultra Sun It will pick up anything it finds on the ground. Sometimes it finds coins, so Murkrow and Meowth will go after it. Ultra Moon An astonishingly cowardly PokÃ©mon, Wimpod will whir its legs at high speeds to run away if anything comes near it. Generation VIII Galar #232 Isle of Armor #124 Crown Tundra #â€” Sinnoh #â€” Hisui #â€” This PokÃ©mon has no PokÃ©dex entries in Brilliant Diamond, Shining Pearl , and Legends: Arceus . Sword It's nature's cleanerâ€”it eats anything and everything, including garbage and rotten things. The ground near its nest is always clean. Shield Wimpod gather in swarms, constantly on the lookout for danger. They scatter the moment they detect an enemy's presence. Generation IX Paldea #â€” Kitakami #â€” Blueberry #â€” Lumiose #â€” Hyperspace #062 This PokÃ©mon has no PokÃ©dex entries in Scarlet and Violet . Legends: Z-A Its habitat includes everything from beaches to seabeds. It is happy to eat up even rotten scraps, providing a sort of natural cleaning service. Spin-off games Pokopia #â€” This PokÃ©mon has no PokÃ©dex entries in Pokopia . sections.title: PokÃ©dex entries
sections.text: PokÃ©dex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode PokÃ©mon Source Entry SM020 Wimpod Rotom PokÃ©dex Wimpod, the Turn Tail PokÃ©mon. A Bug and Water type. From beaches to seabeds, Wimpod live in all kinds of places. They eat anything, even if it's rotten. Nature's vacuum cleaners!
